title: viola canina subsp. riviniana
intro: Viola canina subsp. riviniana, also known as the wood violet or pale dog violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as rheumatism, arthritis, and digestive problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The roots of Viola canina subsp. riviniana have been used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, to help alleviate symptoms of these conditions, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for overall well-being and natural healing.
